hypothesis: If protein sequence design and structure prediction are trained jointly end-to-end in a discrete stochastic autoencoder with structural reconstruction losses, then the model will generate sequences that recover native residues and fold back to target backbones with higher TM-scores/lower RMSDs—and predict structures more accurately—than decoupled baselines like ProteinMPNN+Boltz-2, because joint optimization enforces sequence–structure self-consistency.
